rationale: The cited paper is a close conceptual predecessor: it examines postpurchase consumption via price bundling/transaction decoupling and sunk-cost mechanisms, which directly matches the citing paper’s stated effort to extend economic/mental-accounting accounts of stockpiling and consumption incidence vs. quantity. With this mechanism-level overlap and an exact DOI match in the reference list (ref 39), this does not look like an obvious incidental mention.
